variant_with_pileup.name: variant_with_pileup
variant_with_pileup.note: make variant track less tall  DONE: Set the volvox_filtered_vcf display height to 60 so the single-row variant track no longer dominates the pileup below. Regenerated + verified.

alignments/read_cloud.name: alignments/read_cloud
alignments/read_cloud.note: UNBLOCKED + fixed: the 'Loading hang' was a STALE-BUILD artifact — the committed jbrowse-web build predated the read-cloud/sashimi commits. After rebuilding from HEAD the samplot read cloud regenerates cleanly (coverage on top, distance-laid-out cloud in the middle on a 1bp/1kb/34kb log axis, pileup below). Set arcColorByType:'insertSize' so the cloud keys purely on insert size — short-insert pairs paint pink (#ffc0cb, confirmed in core theme.ts), long-insert red; the default insertSizeAndOrientation only pinks short pairs that also keep normal orientation, which is why short-insert was hard to see. The discordant SV clusters at this locus (the whole ~50kb volvox-sv ctgA) are long-insert deletion-supporting (red) — the synthetic data is deletion-dominated, so no prominent pink pairs exist HERE, but the pink short-insert path is now active for data that has them.

alignments/haplotype_groupby.name: alignments/haplotype_groupby
alignments/haplotype_groupby.note: fixed: now a two-stage figure (reviewer). Stage 1 = the menu path (track menu -> Group by... submenu, inner item boxed); stage 2 = the Group-by dialog it opens, driven to the 'Tag (HP, RG, ...)' dimension with HP typed in — the dialog shows the live 'Found values: 1, 2' HP preview and the 'Also color reads by this tag' checkbox. Reads start colored by HP (colorBy tag HP) in both frames, satisfying the 'use colorby tag hp to start with' ask. Used XPath selectors to disambiguate the duplicate 'Group by...' label (inner = last match) and the Tag option (parens/commas break the text pseudo-selector). Regenerated + verified.

(2) Added the figure to gene_track.md after the transl_except prose with an explanatory caption. (3) Track rename ("NCBI RefSeq w/ subfeature details") still pending a decision — renaming ncbi_gff_hg19 in config_demo.json touches ~13 figures.  DONE (rename): renamed demo track ncbi_gff_hg19 "NCBI RefSeq w/ subfeature details" -> "NCBI RefSeq genes (hg19)" in config_demo.json; force-regenerated all 14 figures that show its label (sibling ncbi_gff_hg19_2 untouched, used in 0 figures). jbrowse-img/dark_theme.name: jbrowse-img/dark_theme
jbrowse-img/dark_theme.note: fixed (source): worker-side feature label/description colors are baked at feature-fetch time from session.themeOptions, but jb2export only applied --themeName at rasterization (after fetch), so descriptions used the light-theme dark blue (illegible on dark). renderRegion.tsx now calls session.setThemeName(themeName) up front before any fetch. Gene descriptions now render in the dark-tuned light blue (blue[300]). Regenerated + synced.

jbrowse-img/hg38_hs1_mm39_synteny.name: jbrowse-img/hg38_hs1_mm39_synteny
jbrowse-img/hg38_hs1_mm39_synteny.note: resolved: not manual (autogenerated by @jbrowse/img). Re-rendered — autoDiagonalize now applies; the hs1<->mm39 lower level reorders from a crossing hairball into legible diagonal blocks (top hg38<->hs1 level stays near-vertical). cigar indels not visible at whole-genome scale.

jbrowse-img/methylation.name: jbrowse-img/methylation

jbrowse-img/hs1_mm39_synteny.name: jbrowse-img/hs1_mm39_synteny
jbrowse-img/hs1_mm39_synteny.note: resolved: not manual (autogenerated by @jbrowse/img). Re-rendered — autoDiagonalize now applies in jb2export SSR (committed PNG predated it), so mm39 chromosomes reorder and the ribbons read cleanly. cigar indels are not drawn at whole-genome scale, so the cigar-off ask is moot here.

jbrowse-img/grape_peach_synteny.name: jbrowse-img/grape_peach_synteny
jbrowse-img/grape_peach_synteny.note: TAGGING FIXED (autogenerated by @jbrowse/img, not manual). OPACITY: investigated — re-rendering with the current jb2export turns the dense thin-anchor PAF into faint pastel ribbons (the old vivid filled-trapezoid look came from an older synteny renderer; drawCurves/alpha/autoDiagonalize all tested, all fainter). Kept the original committed PNG rather than regress it; a proper opacity tweak needs the renderer to fill trapezoids at moderate alpha for this dataset.
